metadata.scheme_name: Provision of Special Assistance for Development of Low-Cost Educational Materials (SIPDA)

raw_data.details: The scheme "Provision of Special Assistance for Development of Low-Cost Educational Materials (SIPDA)" under the Scheme for Implementation of the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016 (SIPDA), aims to financially support the creation and modernization of assistive educational tools for visually impaired students pursuing school and higher education. Through this scheme, implementing agencies receive grants to procure, produce, and enhance the capacity of institutions producing educational aids such as Braille presses and digital books. The scheme is implemented by the National Institute for the Empowerment of Persons with Visual Disabilities (NIEPVD).
raw_data.benefits: Support for Braille Presses: Assistance is given for the installation of new Braille presses and enhancement of the capacity and modernization of existing Braille presses. Upgradation of Talking Book Studios: Financial support is offered for increasing the capacity and modernization of existing Talking Book Studios. Creation and Enhancement of Digital Book Production Centers: Grants are available for setting up new Digital Book Production Centers and modernizing existing centers to improve production capabilities. Support for Large Print Book Production: Funding is provided for establishing new Large Print Book Production Centers and increasing the capacity and modernization of existing large print facilities.
raw_data.eligibility: Individual Eligibility:The applicant must be a visually impaired student enrolled in school or pursuing higher education. The applicant must have a valid Unique Disability ID (UDID) card issued by the competent authority. Institutional Eligibility:The agency must be approved for SIPDA projects. The agency must be actively involved in the development, production, or distribution of educational materials for persons with visual disabilities.
raw_data.application_process:
OfflineFor Institutional Applicants:
Step 1: The implementing agency should prepare a project proposal outlining the infrastructure to be established or upgraded.
Step 2: The proposal should be submitted to NIEPVD or the concerned state authority for review and approval.
Step 3: Once approved, funds are disbursed as per the sanctioned amount.
Step 4: The agency must maintain transparency in utilization and submit reports and utilization certificates as required. For Student Beneficiaries (if applicable):
Step 1: The student should approach an approved implementing agency.
Step 2: Submit necessary documents such as Aadhaar, UDID, and school/college ID.
Step 3: The agency will facilitate access to learning materials based on availability and eligibility. Website: [niepvd.nic.in](https://niepvd.nic.in/)Telephone No.: 7579278149
raw_data.documents_required: For Students:Unique Disability ID (UDID)Date of BirthParent/Guardian's Mobile NumberSchool DetailsFor Implementing Agencies:Project Proposal outlining the development and production of education support materials. Relevant Financial Statements (for recurring and non-recurring grant applications). Any other documents as required.

Frequently Asked QuestionsWhat is the objective of the scheme "Support for Development of Educational Materials (SIPDA)"? The objective of the scheme "Support for Development of Educational Materials (SIPDA)" is to provide financial assistance to authorized implementing agencies for developing, modernizing, and enhancing the infrastructure needed to produce educational materials for visually impaired students. Who is eligible to apply for assistance under this scheme? The eligible applicants under this scheme include implementing agencies that are approved and operational in producing educational resources such as Braille books, talking books, digital books, and large print books for visually impaired school-going and higher education students. What types of organizations can receive the grant assistance under this scheme? Grant assistance is provided to operational agencies or institutions that are involved in the production and development of accessible educational materials and are recognized as implementing agencies by the concerned authority. What specific types of infrastructure are supported under this scheme? This scheme supports the installation of new Braille presses, modernization of existing Braille presses, upgradation of Talking Book Studios, establishment of digital book production centers, and development of large print production units. Is the assistance provided under this scheme recurring or one-time? The assistance under this scheme is primarily non-recurring in nature and is meant for the establishment or modernization of infrastructure rather than for recurring operational expenses. What kind of modernization activities are covered for existing facilities? The modernization activities include upgrading production equipment, enhancing technical capacity, and digital transformation of the infrastructure used for producing educational content for visually impaired students. Are private organizations eligible for financial assistance under the scheme? Private organizations can be eligible only if they are recognized as implementing agencies and meet the scheme’s guidelines for non-recurring grant support. What kind of educational content production is encouraged under the scheme? The scheme encourages the production of Braille books, talking books, digital books, and large print books specifically designed to cater to the learning needs of visually impaired students. Does the scheme include any support for training or capacity building? No, the scheme is focused on the infrastructure and production side of accessible educational materials and does not directly cover training or capacity building programs. What are the responsibilities of the implementing agency under this scheme? The implementing agency is responsible for ensuring timely utilization of the financial grant, proper maintenance of the developed infrastructure, and regular production of accessible educational content for eligible students. What is the contact information for further queries related to the scheme? For further information or clarification regarding the scheme, interested applicants can contact the helpline number 7579278149 or visit the official [NIEPVD](https://niepvd.nic.in/)website. raw_data.scheme_name: Provision of Special Assistance for Development of Low-Cost Educational Materials (SIPDA)

faqs.question: What is the objective of the scheme "Support for Development of Educational Materials (SIPDA)"?
faqs.answer: The objective of the scheme "Support for Development of Educational Materials (SIPDA)" is to provide financial assistance to authorized implementing agencies for developing, modernizing, and enhancing the infrastructure needed to produce educational materials for visually impaired students.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for assistance under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The eligible applicants under this scheme include implementing agencies that are approved and operational in producing educational resources such as Braille books, talking books, digital books, and large print books for visually impaired school-going and higher education students.

faqs.question: What types of organizations can receive the grant assistance under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Grant assistance is provided to operational agencies or institutions that are involved in the production and development of accessible educational materials and are recognized as implementing agencies by the concerned authority.

faqs.question: What specific types of infrastructure are supported under this scheme?
faqs.answer: This scheme supports the installation of new Braille presses, modernization of existing Braille presses, upgradation of Talking Book Studios, establishment of digital book production centers, and development of large print production units.

faqs.question: Is the assistance provided under this scheme recurring or one-time?
faqs.answer: The assistance under this scheme is primarily non-recurring in nature and is meant for the establishment or modernization of infrastructure rather than for recurring operational expenses.

faqs.question: What kind of modernization activities are covered for existing facilities?
faqs.answer: The modernization activities include upgrading production equipment, enhancing technical capacity, and digital transformation of the infrastructure used for producing educational content for visually impaired students.

faqs.question: Are private organizations eligible for financial assistance under the scheme?
faqs.answer: Private organizations can be eligible only if they are recognized as implementing agencies and meet the scheme’s guidelines for non-recurring grant support.

faqs.question: What kind of educational content production is encouraged under the scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme encourages the production of Braille books, talking books, digital books, and large print books specifically designed to cater to the learning needs of visually impaired students.

faqs.question: Does the scheme include any support for training or capacity building?
faqs.answer: No, the scheme is focused on the infrastructure and production side of accessible educational materials and does not directly cover training or capacity building programs.

faqs.question: What are the responsibilities of the implementing agency under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The implementing agency is responsible for ensuring timely utilization of the financial grant, proper maintenance of the developed infrastructure, and regular production of accessible educational content for eligible students.

faqs.question: What is the contact information for further queries related to the scheme?
faqs.answer: For further information or clarification regarding the scheme, interested applicants can contact the helpline number 7579278149 or visit the official [NIEPVD](https://niepvd.nic.in/)website.
